# Bullet Rust SDK

> Rust SDK for the Bullet perpetual futures DEX. Supports REST, WebSocket, and on-chain transaction signing via Ed25519.

This file provides everything an LLM or agent needs to build a trading bot, integration, or tool on top of the Bullet exchange using the `bullet-rust-sdk` crate.

## Quick Start

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::{
    Client, Keypair, Network, Transaction, CallMessage, UserAction,
    Side, OrderType, MarketId, NewOrderArgs, PositiveDecimal,
    UserActionDiscriminants, Topic, OrderbookDepth, ServerMessage,
};

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    // 1. Create keypair and client
    let keypair = Keypair::from_hex("0x_your_private_key_hex")?;
    let client = Client::builder()
        .network(Network::Testnet) // or Network::Mainnet
        .keypair(keypair.clone())
        .user_actions(vec![
            UserActionDiscriminants::PlaceOrders,
            UserActionDiscriminants::CancelOrders,
            UserActionDiscriminants::CancelMarketOrders,
        ])
        .build()
        .await?;

    // 2. Fetch market info (symbol -> MarketId mapping)
    let info = client.exchange_info().await?.into_inner();
    let btc_market_id: u16 = info.symbols.iter()
        .find(|s| s.symbol == "BTC-USD" && s.status == "TRADING")
        .map(|s| s.market_id)
        .expect("BTC-USD not found");

    // 3. Fetch orderbook
    let book = client.order_book(Some(10), "BTC-USD").await?.into_inner();
    // book.bids: Vec<Vec<String>> -- each entry is [price, quantity]
    // book.asks: Vec<Vec<String>> -- each entry is [price, quantity]

    // 4. Place an order (convenience method)
    let resp = client.place_orders(
        MarketId(btc_market_id),
        vec![NewOrderArgs {
            price: PositiveDecimal::try_from(rust_decimal::Decimal::from(50000))?,
            size: PositiveDecimal::try_from(rust_decimal::Decimal::new(1, 3))?, // 0.001
            side: Side::Bid,       // Bid = buy, Ask = sell
            order_type: OrderType::PostOnly,
            reduce_only: false,
            client_order_id: None,
            pending_tpsl_pair: None,
        }],
        false, // replace
        None,  // sub_account_index
    ).await?;
    println!("TX submitted: id={}, status={:?}", resp.id, resp.status);

    // 5. Cancel all orders on a market
    client.cancel_market_orders(MarketId(btc_market_id), None).await?;

    Ok(())
}
```

## Architecture

The SDK is a single crate (`bullet-rust-sdk`) that re-exports all commonly-needed types at the crate root. You never need to add internal crates as direct dependencies:

- `bullet-exchange-interface` -- On-chain types (UserAction, Side, OrderType, MarketId, etc). Accessible via `bullet_rust_sdk::*` (flat re-exports) or `bullet_rust_sdk::types::bullet_exchange_interface` (full path).
- `bullet-ws-interface` -- WebSocket message types (DepthUpdate, BookTickerMessage, etc). Access via `bullet_rust_sdk::types::*` or `bullet_rust_sdk::ServerMessage`.

## Dependency

```toml
# In Cargo.toml -- use git dependency with a pinned rev:
bullet-rust-sdk = { git = "https://github.com/bulletxyz/bullet-rust-sdk.git", rev = "...", default-features = false }

# Or path for local development:
bullet-rust-sdk = { path = "../bullet-rust-sdk/rust", default-features = false }
```

The SDK requires `edition = "2024"` and uses `tokio` for async.

## Important Terminology

| Bullet SDK term | Common term | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `Side::Bid` | Buy | SDK uses Bid/Ask, not Buy/Sell |
| `Side::Ask` | Sell | |
| `MarketId(u16)` | Symbol ID | Numeric ID, not a string. Resolve via `exchange_info()` |
| `PositiveDecimal` | Decimal (>0) | Newtype over `rust_decimal::Decimal`. Use `PositiveDecimal::try_from(decimal)` |
| `OrderType::PostOnly` | Maker order | |
| `OrderType::ImmediateOrCancel` | Market order | Standard across perp exchanges -- market orders are IoC |
| `OrderType::Limit` | Limit order | |
| `CallMessage` | Transaction payload | Wraps a `UserAction` for signing |

## Common Imports

```rust
// Everything you need for a typical trading bot:
use bullet_rust_sdk::{
    // Core client
    Client, Keypair, Network,
    // Trading types
    Side, OrderType, MarketId, OrderId, ClientOrderId,
    NewOrderArgs, CancelOrderArgs, AmendOrderArgs, PositiveDecimal,
    // Transaction building (only needed for manual CallMessage construction)
    Transaction, CallMessage, UserAction, UserActionDiscriminants,
    // WebSocket
    ServerMessage, Topic, OrderbookDepth, KlineInterval,
    // Errors
    SDKError, SDKResult, WSErrors,
    // Response types
    SubmitTxResponse,
};

// Generated REST response types (for type annotations)
use bullet_rust_sdk::codegen::types::{
    Account, Balance, Symbol, BinanceOrder, OrderBook, ExchangeInfo,
};

// WebSocket message types (for pattern matching)
use bullet_rust_sdk::types::{
    DepthUpdate, AggTradeMessage, BookTickerMessage, MarkPriceMessage,
    OrderUpdateMessage, OrderUpdateData,
};
```

## REST API Methods

All methods are on `Client` (via `Deref` to the generated Progenitor client). They take **positional arguments**, not builder pattern. Responses require `.into_inner()` to unwrap the response envelope.

### Market Data

```rust
// Exchange info -- symbols, market IDs, filters
let info = client.exchange_info().await?.into_inner();
// info.symbols: Vec<Symbol>
//   Symbol { symbol: String, market_id: u16, status: String, base_asset: String,
//            price_precision: u8, quantity_precision: u8, filters: Vec<Value>, ... }

// Orderbook
let book = client.order_book(Some(20), "BTC-USD").await?.into_inner();
// book.bids: Vec<Vec<String>> -- [[price, qty], ...]
// book.asks: Vec<Vec<String>> -- [[price, qty], ...]
// book.last_update_id: u64

// Ticker
let price = client.ticker_price("BTC-USD").await?.into_inner();
// price.price: Decimal, price.symbol: String

// Funding rate
let rate = client.funding_rate(None, None, "BTC-USD").await?.into_inner();
```

### Account Data

```rust
let address = format!("0x{}", keypair.public_key_hex());

// Account info (positions, margins)
let account = client.account_info(&address).await?.into_inner();
// account.positions: Vec<AccountPosition>
//   AccountPosition { symbol, position_amt: Decimal, entry_price: Decimal,
//                     unrealized_profit: Decimal, market_id: u16, ... }

// Balances
let balances = client.account_balance(&address).await?.into_inner();
// Vec<Balance> { asset: String, balance: Decimal, available_balance: Decimal, ... }

// Open orders
let orders = client.query_open_orders(&address, "BTC-USD").await?.into_inner();
// Vec<BinanceOrder> { order_id: u64, symbol: String, side: String, price: Decimal,
//                     orig_qty: Decimal, executed_qty: Decimal, order_type: String,
//                     client_order_id: Option<String>, status: String, ... }
```

## Order Operations

### Convenience Methods (recommended)

The simplest way to place and cancel orders. These handle CallMessage construction, transaction signing, and submission internally.

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::*;

// Place orders
let resp = client.place_orders(
    MarketId(market_id),
    vec![NewOrderArgs {
        price: PositiveDecimal::try_from(price_decimal)?,
        size: PositiveDecimal::try_from(qty_decimal)?,
        side: Side::Bid,
        order_type: OrderType::PostOnly,
        reduce_only: false,
        client_order_id: Some(ClientOrderId(12345)),
        pending_tpsl_pair: None,
    }],
    false,  // replace: true = cancel-and-replace existing orders
    None,   // sub_account_index: None = main account
).await?;

// Cancel specific orders
let resp = client.cancel_orders(
    MarketId(market_id),
    vec![CancelOrderArgs {
        order_id: Some(OrderId(12345)),
        client_order_id: None,
    }],
    None,
).await?;

// Cancel all orders on a market
client.cancel_market_orders(MarketId(market_id), None).await?;

// Cancel all orders across all markets
client.cancel_all_orders(None).await?;

// Amend (cancel + replace) orders atomically
let resp = client.amend_orders(
    MarketId(market_id),
    vec![AmendOrderArgs {
        cancel: CancelOrderArgs {
            order_id: Some(OrderId(12345)),
            client_order_id: None,
        },
        place: NewOrderArgs {
            price: PositiveDecimal::try_from(new_price)?,
            size: PositiveDecimal::try_from(new_qty)?,
            side: Side::Bid,
            order_type: OrderType::Limit,
            reduce_only: false,
            client_order_id: None,
            pending_tpsl_pair: None,
        },
    }],
    None,
).await?;
```

### Manual CallMessage Construction (advanced)

For full control over transaction parameters, construct `CallMessage` directly:

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::{CallMessage, UserAction, Transaction};

let call_msg = CallMessage::User(UserAction::PlaceOrders {
    market_id: MarketId(market_id),
    orders: vec![NewOrderArgs { /* ... */ }],
    replace: false,
    sub_account_index: None,
});

let signed = Transaction::builder()
    .call_message(call_msg)
    .max_fee(10_000_000)        // override client default
    .signer(&other_keypair)     // override client keypair
    .client(&client)
    .build()?;

let resp = client.send_transaction(&signed).await?;
```

## WebSocket Streaming

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::{Topic, OrderbookDepth, ServerMessage};
use bullet_rust_sdk::ws::models::TaggedMessage;

// Connect
let mut ws = client.connect_ws().call().await?;

// Subscribe to topics
ws.subscribe([
    Topic::depth("BTC-USD", OrderbookDepth::D20),   // 20-level orderbook
    Topic::book_ticker("BTC-USD"),                   // best bid/ask
    Topic::agg_trade("BTC-USD"),                     // trades
    Topic::mark_price("BTC-USD"),                    // mark price + funding rate
], None).await?;

// Receive events
loop {
    match ws.recv().await? {
        ServerMessage::DepthUpdate(d) => {
            // d.symbol: String
            // d.bids: Vec<PriceLevel> -- PriceLevel(String, String) = (price, qty)
            // d.asks: Vec<PriceLevel>
            // d.last_update_id: u64
        }
        ServerMessage::BookTicker(bt) => {
            // bt.symbol, bt.best_bid_price, bt.best_bid_qty,
            // bt.best_ask_price, bt.best_ask_qty: all String
            // bt.update_id: u64
        }
        ServerMessage::AggTrade(t) => {
            // t.symbol, t.price, t.quantity: String
            // t.side: String ("BUY" or "SELL")
            // t.order_id: u64, t.trade_id: u64
            // t.user_address: String (hex)
            // t.is_buyer_maker: bool
            // t.fee: String, t.net_fee: String
        }
        ServerMessage::MarkPrice(mp) => {
            // mp.symbol, mp.mark_price, mp.index_price, mp.funding_rate: String
        }
        ServerMessage::OrderUpdate(ou) => {
            // ou.order: OrderUpdateData enum:
            //   TradeFill { common, side, price, quantity, last_filled_qty, ... }
            //   PlaceOrder { common, side, price, quantity, ... }
            //   Cancel { common, ... }
            // common: { symbol, order_id: u64, status: String, client_order_id, ... }
            // status values: "NEW", "PARTIALLY_FILLED", "FILLED", "CANCELED", "EXPIRED"
        }
        ServerMessage::Tagged(TaggedMessage::Pong(_)) => { /* keepalive */ }
        ServerMessage::Tagged(_) => { /* subscribe acks, etc */ }
        ServerMessage::Unknown(err, raw) => {
            eprintln!("Parse error: {err}, raw: {raw}");
        }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

### WebSocket Order Submission

Orders can also be submitted via WebSocket for lower latency:

```rust
// Build and sign the transaction
let signed = Transaction::builder()
    .call_message(call_msg)
    .client(&client)
    .build()?;

// Submit via WebSocket (handles base64 encoding internally)
ws.place_order(&signed, None).await?;
ws.cancel_order(&cancel_signed, None).await?;
```

### WebSocket Reconnection

`WebsocketHandle` returns `Err(WSErrors::WsClosed { .. })` or `Err(WSErrors::WsStreamEnded)` when the connection drops. Reconnect by calling `client.connect_ws().call().await` and re-subscribing.

### WebSocket Thread Safety

`WebsocketHandle` is `Send` but NOT `Sync`. For sharing across async tasks, wrap in `tokio::sync::Mutex`:

```rust
let ws = Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(client.connect_ws().call().await?));
```

For high-throughput bots, dedicate one task to the WebSocket and use `tokio::sync::mpsc` channels to communicate with other tasks.

## Error Handling

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::{SDKError, WSErrors};

// REST errors
match client.order_book(Some(10), "BTC-USD").await {
    Ok(resp) => { /* use resp.into_inner() */ }
    Err(e) => {
        let sdk_err: SDKError = e.into();
        match &sdk_err {
            SDKError::ApiError(resp) if resp.is_retryable() => { /* 429 or 5xx */ }
            SDKError::HttpError(_) => { /* network error, retry */ }
            SDKError::SchemaOutdated => { /* SDK version mismatch, update SDK */ }
            _ => { /* non-retryable */ }
        }
    }
}

// WebSocket errors
match ws.recv().await {
    Err(WSErrors::WsClosed { code, reason }) => { /* reconnect */ }
    Err(WSErrors::WsStreamEnded) => { /* reconnect */ }
    Err(WSErrors::WsConnectionTimeout) => { /* retry with longer timeout */ }
    _ => {}
}
```

## Schema Validation

The client validates that the local SDK schema matches the remote exchange schema on `Client::builder().build()`. If the exchange has been updated and the SDK is out of date, you'll get `SDKError::SchemaOutdated`.

To restrict validation to only the actions you use (avoiding false failures from unrelated schema changes):

```rust
use bullet_rust_sdk::UserActionDiscriminants;

let client = Client::builder()
    .network(Network::Mainnet)
    .user_actions(vec![
        UserActionDiscriminants::PlaceOrders,
        UserActionDiscriminants::CancelOrders,
        UserActionDiscriminants::CancelMarketOrders,
    ])
    .build()
    .await?;
```

## Available OrderType Variants

| Variant | Value | Use Case |
|---|---|---|
| `Limit` | 0 | Standard limit order |
| `PostOnly` | 1 | Maker-only, rejected if it would take |
| `FillOrKill` | 2 | Fill entirely or cancel |
| `ImmediateOrCancel` | 3 | Fill what you can, cancel rest. Use for market orders |

Use `ImmediateOrCancel` with an aggressive slippage price for market orders. This is the same pattern Hyperliquid uses -- their L1 has no native market order type either; the SDK sends a limit IoC at `mid * (1 +/- slippage%)`. Note: Binance futures does have a first-class `MARKET` type where you send no price, but most on-chain perp exchanges (Bullet, Hyperliquid) use IoC instead.

## Available WebSocket Topics

| Topic | Constructor | Data |
|---|---|---|
| Orderbook depth | `Topic::depth("BTC-USD", OrderbookDepth::D20)` | `ServerMessage::DepthUpdate` |
| Best bid/ask | `Topic::book_ticker("BTC-USD")` | `ServerMessage::BookTicker` |
| Trades | `Topic::agg_trade("BTC-USD")` | `ServerMessage::AggTrade` |
| Mark price | `Topic::mark_price("BTC-USD")` | `ServerMessage::MarkPrice` |
| Candles | `Topic::kline("BTC-USD", KlineInterval::H1)` | `ServerMessage::Kline` |
| Liquidations | `Topic::force_order("BTC-USD")` | `ServerMessage::ForceOrder` |
| All tickers | `Topic::all_tickers()` | Mini-ticker for all symbols |
| All mark prices | `Topic::all_mark_prices()` | Mark price for all symbols |

`OrderbookDepth` variants: `D5`, `D10`, `D20`.
`KlineInterval` variants: `M1`, `M5`, `M15`, `M30`, `H1`, `H4`, `D1`.

## Networks

| Network | REST URL | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Network::Mainnet` | `https://tradingapi.bullet.xyz` | Production |
| `Network::Testnet` | `https://tradingapi.testnet.bullet.xyz` | Paper trading / testing |
| `Network::from("https://custom-url")` | Custom URL | Self-hosted or staging |
